visual_features.overall_style: Conservative, academic and slightly dated corporate style. Flat design with minimal decoration, muted greys and a single red brand accent. Focus is on readability and seriousness rather than visual flourish.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by size, weight and color. The logo block and horizontal navigation form the first level. The article title is the main focal point below the fold, using a larger, bolder font than body text. Red text (author name, small icons) is used selectively to emphasize secondary information. Sidebar headings are slightly smaller than the article title but bolder than body text, making them clear section labels.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate whitespace. There is generous vertical space between the global header and the start of the article, and sufficient line spacing in paragraphs. Left and right margins inside the main content column are comfortable, but the overall layout is still fairly content-dense rather than ultra-spacious.
unique_elements: Logo composed of a 4×4 grid of circular dots in grey and red, visually referencing data or indicators.
Two-tone header stack: a very dark grey strip at the top and a large light-grey band for logo and navigation, separated by another dark rule.
Language selector isolated and centered beneath the nav, visually detached from both header and content.
Sidebar newsletter block with inline form and a vertical book-shelf image reinforcing the academic theme.
Small square icon with red play/flag motif next to numbered article paragraphs, used as a visual bullet.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column fixed-width grid (centered content area with main column plus right sidebar; typical of older WordPress/academic themes).
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Standard top horizontal header with centered navigation, followed by a two-column content layout (main article column on the left, narrower sidebar on the right).
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top: stacked bars (dark grey, then light grey) containing logo and nav. Below: a standalone, centered language dropdown. Main body: a large white content area divided into left article column and right sidebar column. Sidebar holds newsletter form and image content stacked vertically. Article column is a linear stream: title, metadata, author line, then paragraphs.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall center-aligned page frame with content columns left-aligned within. Navigation items are horizontally centered within the header width. Text within columns is left-aligned; form labels are left-aligned above inputs.
responsive_hints: Centered fixed-width container suggests desktop-first design; columns likely collapse to single column on narrow screens.
Navigation is a simple horizontal list that could stack into vertical items or a basic dropdown at smaller widths.
Forms and inputs are full-width relative to the sidebar column, easing responsiveness.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. Header bar with logo on the left, nav items aligned on a single horizontal line. Below that, the language dropdown is centered as a separate row. The main section is a two-column layout: left column (~65–70% width) contains article title, date, author, and a numbered article body. Right column (~30–35% width) acts as a sidebar: first a newsletter heading and subscription form, then a book image block and likely additional widgets below the visible fold.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Header band consumes roughly 15–20% of the vertical viewport, with substantial vertical padding around logo and navigation. The main content area dominates remaining space, with the article column taking the majority width. The sidebar is narrower but visually significant due to stacked blocks and imagery. Margins around the main central container leave small gutters to the browser edges; internal padding between header/content/sidebar appears to be in the 16–32px range.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately dense, text-heavy layout typical of academic sites. Paragraphs are long, and the sidebar is information-rich (form, labels, image) but separated enough not to feel cluttered. Not minimalist, but still readable due to line spacing and margins.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Primary flow is vertical with a subtle Z-pattern: user scans from logo at top-left across navigation, then down to the centered language dropdown, then to the article title on the left. From the title, the eye moves through metadata and the first paragraph, then occasionally shifts right to the sidebar newsletter block and image before continuing down the article text.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Rectangular buttons with slightly rounded corners (approx. 2–4px radius). Flat or very light gradient grey background (#E0E0E0–#F0F0F0) with a 1px mid-grey border (#CCCCCC). Text is medium-weight dark grey/black, usually in a standard sans-serif. Padding is modest (about 8px vertical, 16px horizontal), resulting in compact, utilitarian call-to-action elements. No prominent shadows; clearly flat design.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implemented either as <button> elements or <input type='submit'> within forms. For navigation-style CTAs, links may be styled as buttons using <a> tags with button classes. Buttons sit inline with form fields or below them, contained within simple form wrappers without complex nested structures.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Sidebar widgets behave like simple cards: white background on a slightly grey page, often distinguished only by vertical spacing and sometimes a subtle top/bottom margin. No visible heavy shadows; separation relies on whitespace, headings, and content. Internal padding around headings and form fields is moderate (~16px). Corners are square; design is very flat and utilitarian.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Typically implemented as
or
wrappers. Inside: a heading (

or

) followed by content such as forms or images. For image cards (like the bookshelf photo), structure is usually an followed by optional caption text. No separate 'header bar' styling, just a bold heading inside the same box.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Form elements are minimalist. Labels appear above the input fields, left-aligned. Text inputs have a white background with a light grey border (#CCCCCC) and subtle corner rounding (around 3–4px). No inner shadows; flat appearance. The input height is moderate (~32–36px). There is small vertical spacing between label and field (~4–8px), and slightly larger spacing between stacked fields (~12–16px). Validation styling is not visible, but likely uses color changes on the border and small inline messages.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms use a stacked layout:

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal top navigation bar. Menu items are plain text links evenly spaced in a single line on a light grey header. Text color is dark grey, with no visible borders between items. Active or hover states are likely indicated by a color change to the brand red and possibly underline/remove underline. The overall look is flat and understated, matching the academic tone.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Classic unordered list:
  • Item
    • ...
. The UL is horizontally displayed via inline-block or flex. Items have padding on left/right to create clickable targets. For 'Área Reservada' and 'Login', there may be additional classes to distinguish them, but structurally they remain LI > A elements.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Article content uses numbered paragraphs and textual lists. Numbers (1., 2., 3., ...) appear in the text stream rather than in high-styled bullets. A small square icon with a red play/flag symbol is placed to the left of some numbered points, providing a subtle visual anchor. List density is medium; there is a standard line height and minimal extra spacing between points.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Content is likely structured as regular paragraphs (

) with manual numbering rather than semantic

    /
  1. , although an ordered list is also possible. Where icons appear, they may be placed via inline or background-image on a span preceding the paragraph text. Sidebar lists (if any beyond screenshot) are probably simple
      with plain text items separated by vertical spacing rather than borders.

heading.visual_style: Neutral sans-serif headings with moderate stroke contrast and a corporate, no-nonsense feel. The logo wordmark appears serifed, but UI headings (article title, sidebar titles, nav) are sans, with clean straight terminals and standard proportions.

body.visual_style: Readable, neutral sans-serif body text, likely a system font stack (Arial/Helvetica). Medium x-height, unobtrusive appearance suitable for long academic paragraphs.

spacing_system.component_padding: Major components (header, main content container, sidebar widgets) use padding in the 16–24px range (2–3 × base unit) on all sides, with slightly less padding on tighter form elements.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical separation between major sections (header to content, content blocks, sidebar widgets) is around 24–32px, maintaining clear but not excessive gaps.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change text color to the brand red (#C03030) and may add/remove text underline on hover.
Buttons probably darken or lighten slightly on hover via a background-color change, with cursor: pointer.
Language dropdown relies on native browser interactions; enhanced styling might include a subtle focus outline and border-color change.
Links inside content (e.g., author citation) use red text; hover can be reinforced with underline and/or a small color shift.
Transitions, if added, should be subtle 150–200ms color and background transitions to preserve the academic, serious tone. No large-scale animations or parallax are implied by the current design.
